titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Robert Marsham- Townshend Papers
titlestmt.author: by Diana Smith

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

PUBLIC "-//Yale University::Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library//TEXT (US::CtYBR::::[ROBERT MARSHAM-TOWNSHEND PAPERS ])//EN" "marsham.xml" converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 by v1to02.xsl (sy2003-10-15).

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Letters and diaries belonging to Robert Marsham-Townshend containing descriptions of his travels around the world. Many of the letters are written to him, though there is a group of 22 letters written by him to a relative named Harriet, dated 1856-1864. Correspondents include Henry Alabaster, interpreter to the King in Siam; scientists W. S. Atkinson and Robert Stirling; George Ferguson Bowen, first governor of Queensland, Australia; in addition to numerous family members and other acquaintances. Stirling writes at length from the Sandwich Islands describing earthquakes and volcanic eruptions, a particular interest of Marsham-Townshend's. There are also fifteen letters from Youhannah El Karey, a missionary from Nablous, near Jaffa, Palestine. The 18 diaries span the dates 1853-1876, and carry descriptions of travel to Brazil, Egypt, Hong Kong, the Philippines, China, Japan, Argentina, Peru, Bolivia, Chile, Panama, El Salvador, Honduras, California, Australia, Indonesia, Siam, India, Persia, the Middle East, South Africa, Russia, and Europe. There is a great deal of geographic and volcanic observation, as well as descriptions of cities, cultures, and peoples encountered. Accompanied by a bound notebook with notes on volcanoes throughout the world, two passports for Marsham-Townshend to use in Persia, and three contracts and receipts for hiring mules and drivers in Persia.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Charles Apfelbaum on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2001.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Robert Marsham-Townshend Papers are the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robert Marsham-Townshend Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

bioghist.head: ROBERT MARSHAM-TOWNSHEND (1834-1914)
bioghist.p: Robert Marsham, later Robert Marsham-Townshend, British world traveller, was the second son of Charles Marsham, 2nd Earl of Romney. He was a fellow of the Royal Geographic Society, worked in the Diplomatic Service from 1855 to 1859, and served as Lieutenant in the Kent Militia Artillery from 1859 to 1869. Marsham married Clara Catherine Paley in 1877.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
The Robert Marsham-Townshend Papers consist of letters and diaries containing descriptions of Robert Marsham-Townshend's travels around the world. The collection is arranged into three series:
Correspondence
,
Diaries
, and
Other Papers
, and spans the dates 1853-1910.

Many of the letters are written to him, though there is a group of 22 letters written by him to a relative identified only as Harriet, dated 1856-1864. Correspondents include Henry Alabaster, interpreter to the King in Siam; scientists W. S. Atkinson and Robert Stirling; George Ferguson Bowen, first governor of Queensland, Australia; in addition to numerous family members and other acquaintances. Stirling writes at length from the Sandwich Islands describing earthquakes and volcanic eruptions, a particular interest of Marsham-Townshend's. There are also fifteen letters from Youhannah El Karey, a missionary from Nablous, near Jaffa, Palestine.

The 18 diaries span the dates 1853-1876, and carry descriptions of travel to Brazil, Egypt, Hong Kong, the Philippines, China, Japan, Argentina, Peru, Bolivia, Chile, Panama, El Salvador, Honduras, California, Australia, Indonesia, Siam (Thailand), India, Persia (Iran), the Mideast, South Africa, Russia, and Europe. There is a great deal of geographic and volcanic observation, as well as descriptions of cities, cultures, and peoples encountered.

The papers are accompanied by a bound notebook with notes on volcanoes throughout the world, two passports for Marsham-Townshend to use in Persia, and three contracts and receipts for hiring mules and drivers in Persia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I, Correspondence , is organized into Outgoing and Incoming correspondence, and is arranged in alphabetical order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For a letter by C. Hunt dated 1868 Mar 4, see: Marsham, Margaret, folder 16

did.unittitle: Canceled check, carte-de-visite photographs, flower cards, and empty envelopes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Anne Marsham dated 1868 Jul 11 encloses a letter from Charles Marsham, Earl of Romney

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Encloses a letter from C. Hunt, dated 1868 Mar 4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For a letter by Charles Marsham, Earl of Romney dated Jul 11 1868, see: Anne Marsham, folder 13

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II, Diaries , contains diaries listed in chronological order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paris, Switzerland, Germany, Belgium, Italy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Voyage to Brazil, and his stay there

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of addresses in the front, travel journal kept while traveling in Brazil in back

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Travel to Tocantins River, Brazil October 5 - December 18. Back of book includes drafts of letters dated October 1858 - March 1859

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: France, Egypt, Singapore, Hong Kong, Canton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Japan, China, Singapore, Sri Lanka

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portugal to Brazil and Argentina. List of mail either sent or received at different ports between 1862 and 1868 in front

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Argentina, Chile, Bolivia, Peru

did.unittitle: "Ascent of the Volcano of Arequipa etc. etc. 1863"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diary of two ascents, June 10 and 17 1863, with notes on previous ascents in front of volume in Spanish and English

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Peru, Panama, California, Panama, Guatemala, El Salvador, Honduras

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Australia, New Zealand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Indonesia, Philippines, Siam

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Siam, India. Folded sheet of paper carrying handwritten journal from July 16 to July 27 laid in loose

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: India, Persia, Arabian Peninsula, Russia, Germany

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gibraltar, Spain, Portugal, France

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: France, Italy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1873: France, Mideast, Italy, Austria, Germany. 1876: Mideast, Reunion, Mauritius, Madagascar, South Africa

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Eastern Europe, Italy, Corfu, Majorca, Algeria

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III, Other Papers , contains miscellaneous other writings, documents, and financial papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on volcanoes around the world, with mathematical tables, a sketch of a volcano
